text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE — POST-INDUCTION RESPONSE ASSESSMENT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:         BM-POST-IND-001
Patient Name:        Kowalczyk, Renata
Date of Birth:       [AGE 48 AT PROCEDURE]
Sex:                 Female
Ordering Physician:  Hematology/Oncology Service
Clinical Indication: Post-induction day 28 bone marrow evaluation; assess morphologic
                     response to 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin
                     in known AML, FAB M4 (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H).
                     Prior diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia, FAB M4 morphology,
                     FLT3-ITD VAF 82% (high allelic ratio), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation,
                     normal karyotype 46,XX.
Date of Procedure:   [DATE PENDING SYSTEM ENTRY]
Date of Report:      [DATE PENDING SYSTEM ENTRY]

Specimen Type:       Bilateral posterior iliac crest trephine core biopsy and aspirate smears
Site:                Right and left posterior iliac crest

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Ms. Kowalczyk is a 48-year-old female with de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4
(acute myelomonocytic leukemia), diagnosed approximately 28 days prior to the current
procedure. Presenting white blood cell count was 87,400/microliter with circulating blasts
and Auer rods; bone marrow at diagnosis showed 74% blasts with CD34+/CD117+/CD33+/CD13+/
CD64+/MPO+ immunophenotype. Molecular profile at diagnosis: FLT3-ITD allelic ratio 0.82
(VAF 82%), DNMT3A p.R882H (VAF 47%), NPM1 wild-type, IDH1/IDH2 wild-type, TP53 wild-type;
ELN 2022 risk: Adverse. Cytogenetics: 46,XX normal karyotype.

Patient received standard 7+3 induction chemotherapy: cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day continuous
infusion x 7 days (days 1-7) and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV on days 1-3, followed by
midostaurin 50 mg orally twice daily on days 8-21. Course was complicated by febrile
neutropenia (Escherichia coli bacteremia, pan-sensitive, treated with IV ceftriaxone) and
grade 3 oral mucositis requiring parenteral nutrition. Patient recovered from acute toxicities.
Current bone marrow biopsy is performed on day 28 to assess morphologic response and MRD status.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Received in formalin are two bone marrow core biopsy specimens from the posterior iliac crest
(right and left). The right core measures 1.8 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter; the left core
measures 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. Both cores are tan-white, firm, and
cylindrical with intact cortical and trabecular architecture. Specimens are entirely submitted
for decalcification and paraffin embedding. Hematoxylin and eosin (H&E), periodic acid-Schiff
(PAS), reticulin silver stain, and immunohistochemical studies are performed on representative
sections.

Aspiration yielded adequate spiculated marrow particles with good cellular yield. Multiple
aspirate smears prepared with Wright-Giemsa stain. Additional aspirate material submitted
for flow cytometric immunophenotyping (MRD panel), cytogenetic analysis (G-band karyotype),
FLT3-ITD PCR fragment analysis, DNMT3A R882H ddPCR, and iron stain (Prussian blue).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CELLULARITY:
The trephine biopsy cores show moderately hypocellular marrow with an estimated cellularity
of 30-40% (markedly reduced compared to the pre-treatment biopsy, which showed >95%
cellularity). The reduction in cellularity is consistent with the expected post-chemotherapy
hypoplastic/aplastic marrow state following myeloablative induction therapy at day 28, with
evidence of early hematopoietic recovery.

BLAST ASSESSMENT:
Aspirate smear differential count performed on 500-cell count from multiple fields with
adequate particle sections. Myeloblasts: 2% of nucleated cells (below the 5% threshold
required for residual AML). No monocytic blasts or promonocytes identified in excess.
No Auer rods detected on current smear. The residual non-blast elements include maturing
myeloid precursors (predominantly promyelocytes and myelocytes), scattered erythroid
precursors, and occasional megakaryocytes. Lymphocytes are slightly increased as a proportion
of the differential (18%), consistent with lymphocyte-predominant early hematopoietic
recovery post-chemotherapy.

MYELOID SERIES:
Sparse but morphologically maturing myeloid elements are present, ranging from promyelocytes
through bands and segmented neutrophils. No dysplastic nuclear changes (hypogranulation,
hypolobation, pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly) identified. The myeloid-to-erythroid (M:E) ratio
appears slightly reduced.

ERYTHROID SERIES:
Scattered erythroid islands are present with predominantly early normoblasts; some
orythropoietic islands show mild left-shifted maturation consistent with recovery. No
ringed sideroblasts. No significant dyserythropoiesis (no nuclear bridging, bi-nucleation,
or megaloblastoid change beyond what is expected in a recovering marrow).

MEGAKARYOCYTES:
Megakaryocytes are markedly reduced in number (average 0-1 per 10x field in the biopsy
core). Those present are morphologically normal with adequate nuclear lobulation and no
hypolobation or micromegakaryocytes. No clusters or abnormal localization of immature
precursors (ALIP) identified.

RETICULIN FIBROSIS:
Reticulin silver stain shows MF-0 to MF-1 (focal, fine reticulin fibers without coarse
fibers or collagen deposition), consistent with grade 0-1 reticulin fibrosis; this is
not above baseline for post-chemotherapy recovery and is not a feature of significant
myelofibrosis.

BONE TRABECULAE AND STROMA:
Bone trabeculae are intact without osteoclastic resorption or osteosclerosis. Adipose
tissue is relatively expanded (occupying approximately 55-65% of intertrabecular space)
consistent with hypocellular, post-chemotherapy marrow. No hemophagocytosis, epithelioid
granulomas, or non-hematopoietic infiltrate identified.

IRON STORES:
Prussian blue iron stain shows absent stainable iron stores (grade 0/4), consistent with
previous assessment of decreased iron stores at diagnosis and ongoing anemia in the
setting of chronic transfusion requirements.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on paraffin-embedded core biopsy sections):
  CD34:     Rare scattered positive cells, representing <1% of nucleated marrow cells;
            no clustering or significant increase. No blast aggregates or sheets.
  CD117:    Rare scattered mast cells and early progenitors positive; no excess of
            CD117+ progenitors. No abnormal localization or clustering.
  CD33:     Scattered myeloid elements positive (as expected). Not interpretable for
            blast quantification.
  MPO:      Positive in maturing myeloid elements only; no excess blast-lineage MPO
            staining.
  CD64:     Scattered monocytic elements positive; no excess. No immunophenotypically
            aberrant population.
  Ki-67:    Low proliferation index in non-blast elements, consistent with low mitotic
            activity in a recovering marrow (estimated 4-8% of residual hematopoietic cells).

FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING — MRD PANEL:
  Method:   8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (MPF), high-sensitivity MRD protocol;
            minimum 500,000 events acquired.
  Leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) at diagnosis: CD34+/CD117+/CD33+bright/
  CD13+/CD64+/CD14+/- with dim CD45, HLA-DR+, MPO+.
  Results:  No immunophenotypically aberrant myeloid blast population detected using
            the LAIP identified at diagnosis. Additionally, no "different from normal"
            (DFN) phenotypic population identified that would raise concern for
            immunophenotypic shift.
  MRD:      NEGATIVE
  Sensitivity: 0.01% (1 in 10,000 nucleated marrow cells)
  Interpretation: Flow cytometric MRD negativity achieved following induction
  chemotherapy. This is a favorable prognostic marker.

MOLECULAR STUDIES:
  FLT3-ITD PCR (fragment analysis):
    Method: Capillary electrophoresis-based fragment analysis targeting FLT3 exon
            14-15 junction; sensitivity 0.01% (10^-4).
    Result: FLT3-ITD UNDETECTABLE
    Wild-type FLT3 fragment present (as expected). No ITD peak identified above
    assay sensitivity threshold.
    Interpretation: FLT3-ITD molecularly undetectable; consistent with deep molecular
    remission.

  DNMT3A p.R882H ddPCR:
    Method: Droplet digital PCR targeting DNMT3A c.2645G>A (p.R882H).
    Result: VAF 1.8% (low-level residual signal detected)
    Interpretation: The very low-level DNMT3A R882H signal (VAF 1.8%) is below the
    threshold for residual AML and is consistent with a pre-existing clonal
    hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) clone. DNMT3A R882H at VAF 1-5%
    in the setting of MRD-negative AML complete remission is a recognized CHIP-
    associated finding and does not indicate residual leukemia. Serial monitoring
    for CHIP progression is recommended; this finding does not alter complete
    remission status. Clinical correlation with hematology/oncology recommended.

CYTOGENETICS (conventional G-band karyotype):
  Method:   Standard G-banding on metaphase spreads from cultured aspirate;
            20 metaphases analyzed.
  Result:   46,XX [20/20]
  Interpretation: Normal female karyotype; no cytogenetic evidence of residual
  AML or acquired chromosomal abnormality. Prior abnormality not detected (patient
  was 46,XX at diagnosis; same normal karyotype retained). Cytogenetic complete
  remission confirmed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS / FINAL DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

BONE MARROW, RIGHT AND LEFT POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST, CORE BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE:

  COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION (CR) AFTER FIRST INDUCTION CHEMOTHERAPY

  1. Moderately hypocellular bone marrow (30-40%), consistent with post-chemotherapy
     aplasia with early trilineage hematopoietic recovery.

  2. Blast count 2% by 500-cell morphologic differential — below 5% threshold;
     no Auer rods identified; no morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent AML.

  3. Multiparameter flow cytometry MRD: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%).
     No immunophenotypically aberrant blast population identified using LAIP-based
     and DFN-based MRD strategies.

  4. FLT3-ITD by PCR fragment analysis: UNDETECTABLE (sensitivity 10^-4).
     Deep molecular remission of the FLT3-ITD clone achieved.

  5. DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: Low-level residual signal at VAF 1.8%.
     Consistent with persistence of a pre-existing CHIP clone rather than residual
     AML; does not affect CR designation. See comment.

  6. Cytogenetics: 46,XX [20/20] — normal female karyotype; no acquired cytogenetic
     abnormality identified; cytogenetic complete remission confirmed.

  7. Reticulin fibrosis: MF-0 to MF-1; no significant myelofibrosis.

  8. Decreased marrow iron stores by Prussian blue stain (grade 0/4).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. COMPLETE REMISSION (CR) STATUS: This bone marrow examination demonstrates achievement
   of complete morphologic remission (CR) as defined by ELN 2022 and standard AML
   response criteria: <5% blasts by morphology, absence of Auer rods, no circulating
   blasts (see clinical correlation), and recovery of peripheral blood counts (per
   clinical information). The MRD-negative result by high-sensitivity multiparameter
   flow cytometry confers additional favorable prognostic significance (MRD-negative CR,
   CRi if counts not fully recovered — please correlate with concurrent CBC).

2. FLT3-ITD MRD NEGATIVITY: Molecular clearance of FLT3-ITD by PCR is a favorable
   prognostic finding in the context of high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD AML. In patients
   with high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD and NPM1 wild-type (ELN Adverse risk), achieving
   MRD-negative CR after induction is associated with improved outcomes, particularly
   in the context of planned allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

3. DNMT3A R882H RESIDUAL SIGNAL: The low-level ddPCR signal for DNMT3A R882H (VAF 1.8%)
   must be interpreted in clinical context. At diagnosis, this mutation was present at
   VAF 47%, co-occurring with FLT3-ITD (VAF 82%). The marked reduction from 47% to 1.8%
   and the clearance of FLT3-ITD confirms effective treatment of the AML clone.
   The residual low-level DNMT3A R882H at VAF ~2% likely represents a pre-existing
   CHIP clone (DNMT3A R882H is one of the most common CHIP mutations) that may have
   been present prior to AML development and is now unmasked in the recovering marrow.
   This does not represent residual AML and does not change the CR designation.
   Serial monitoring for clonal dynamics, including DNMT3A VAF trending, is
   recommended at future timepoints.

4. HYPOCELLULARITY: The 30-40% cellularity is expected at day 28 post-myeloablative
   induction with anthracycline/cytarabine-based therapy. Early trilineage hematopoietic
   recovery is underway. Full count recovery may require several additional weeks.

5. IRON DEFICIENCY: Absent stainable iron stores reflect the combined effect of
   hemolysis, pre-existing anemia, and packed red blood cell transfusion-related
   dilution of reticuloendothelial iron stores. Iron supplementation may be considered
   if peripheral iron studies confirm deficiency; clinical correlation recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SIGNATURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Interpreted by: Attending Hematopathologist
Department of Hematopathology
[Signature and credentialing information per institutional policy]

Report Status: FINAL

** ADDENDUM — PENDING STUDIES **
The following study results are PENDING at time of this report and will be issued as
a supplemental addendum upon receipt:
  - Peripheral blood differential and blast count (clinical correlation requested
    to confirm peripheral blood CR criteria — ANC recovery, platelet count)
  - Confirmatory DNMT3A R882H reflex sequencing (Sanger sequencing)
This report is otherwise FINAL for all studies listed above.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONIC SIGN-OUT: COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
